Ht Movement 
‘When a character attacks while standing still or strolling his skill number is not modified. ‘When he is walking or running, its reduced, as the chart indicates. When the target is mov- ing, the attackers skill is also reduced. 
eae) Hit Location When a characteris hit in combat you can, if you like, roll on the Hit Location Table (see Fold-Out 6) to see where his wound is located Use your judgment about this; for instance, i someone is stunned, who cares where the shot hit (if anywhere)? Similarly, if he's dead or vaporized, it's a mite moot, and even if he's incapacitated, he's not going anywhere, so ‘who cares whether it’s a hit in the head or the abdomen? Too, witha flamethrower for exam ple, precise hit location may be irrelevant — the damage may be equally distributed over lots of the body. ‘When a characteris wounded in a patcular location, not only does he suffer a-4 penalty ‘onal skill and attribute rolls, but he loses the use ofthat part of his body, “Lose the use of can mean a number of diferent things ‘Arm: Can't use that arm. Can't carry heavy things, Can't use weapons that require the use of both arms leg: Can't move faster than a walk (hop, hop). No bike riding, either. Chest: Oops. Better sit down. You're not yo- ing anywhere, although you can still fire ‘weapons and use skis, ‘Abdomen: Like chest, only more painful Head: Whoa, Bad news. What “ose the use cof” means is upto you. It ight mean he can't see anything — either because he's blinded Cr because blood is running into his ees. Or ‘maybe it means he’s actually incapacitated Ahead hits are not good). Or maybe “lose the se of" means exactly that and he gets real stupid all of a sudden. 
Multiple-Type Armor 
For the sake of simplicity, we said that ar mor was only of one type. Actually, of course, ‘many kinds of armor do provide some protec tion against more than one type of weapon. saa trivial example, you could buff a suit of plate mail toa fine finish, so it would provide some protection against lasers as well as im- pact weapons. ‘Multiple-type armor just has armor ratings including more than one letter-number com- bination — for instance, that polished plate ‘might be 13/11. Laminated composite battle ar- ‘mor might be L4/13/P2/AP6, We leave this up to you 
—e- 
Drawing, Ammunition, and Reloading Drawing weapons in desperation when am- bushed and hunching dawn behind a rack to reload are valuable dramatic tools in any 
actionadventure game. On the other hand they involve a cena amount of bookkeeping 
I Drawing Weapons {fa citizen does not have a weapon in hand, he cannot use a weapon until he draws one if a weapon is readily available (eg, in a holster or scabbard, slung over the back, et) drawing the weapon takes one combat round IF itis not immediately available, drawing it may take more than one round, at the gamemasters discretion. ia character wishes to replace the weapon he has at hand with another one, he may either drop the weapon he's holding and draw a new o7 one round) or put it away and draw another one {taking a total of two combat rounds), In a round that a characteris drawing or replacing ‘weapon, he may not-make an attack, although hee may dodge or move (but not at sprint speed) 
El Ammunition and Reloading ‘Most ranged weapons come with a limited supply of ammunition, and must be periodically reloaded. The Weapon Chart (see Fold-Out 8) indicates the number of rounds a weapon may be used before reloading is necessary. Each time a player fire a weapon, he notes it as been fed once. When the total ‘number of ings equals the number of rounds ‘of ammunition indicated on the table, the ‘weapon is unloaded and must be reloaded 
before it may fire again. Reloading a weapon takes the same time as drawing a weapon (ie. ‘one round) — assuming, that is, that a spare reload is readily available. If not, reloading ‘means running down to PLC for ammunition, and that’s likely to take considerably more time. 
aa Malfunctions 
Weapons are divided into three types depending on how thoroughly tested they are: Standarduissue, experimental. and really experimental 3 Al the weapons listed on the Weapons CChart (see Fold Out 8) are ether standardissue Cr experimental (asthe table indicates; note that melee weapons'and primitive weapons are considered non-experimental for these pu poses) Anything R&D issues to Troubleshooter Br0Ups specifically forthe purpose of being tested (ee 78) is really experimental Whenever anyone fires. a standard-ssue weapon, and his sil rol is 20, t malfunctions Experimental weapons malfunction on a skill roll of 19 oF 20. Really experimental weapons are so Unreliable that they malfunction whenever you want them tof you want to be “fairer” about it, assign a really experimental weapon a malfunction number — 10, say. Then, whenever the fire's skill roll is greater than or equal to the malfunction number, the ‘weapon malfunctions. in published Paranoia adventures, we tell you when and how really ‘experimental equipment appearing. in. the adventure malfunctions, (See “Into the Out doors with Gun and Camera” for examples.)
